title: auto-apply: classify expired listings, stop them poisoning cooldown queue
description: When auto-apply hits a listing that's been deleted from the platform, the filler raises FormNotFoundError -> apply.py records status=failed -> cooldown_remaining_sec treats it as a real platform-throttle event -> 120s cooldown locks the queue. Every subsequent matching flat is skipped while real platform-side throttling never even happened. Fix: introduce ListingExpiredError, raise it on HTTP 404/410, on Kleinanzeigen redirect away from /s-anzeige/, and on WG-Gesucht missing CTA. apply.py records as 'auto_skipped: listing_expired' so existing cooldown/failures-for-flat exclusions cover it. run_pipeline_apply also excludes such flats from selection for 7 days (TTL chosen over permanent exclusion so a future selector regression self-heals).

title: doctor crashes on legacy DBs missing matched_saved_searches_json
description: The user_id rebuild migration (758a31a) assumes matches has matched_saved_searches_json and applications has triggered_by_saved_search, but the COLUMNS forward-migration that added those was deleted in the same commit. DBs that predate the saved-searches feature crash with OperationalError: no such column: matched_saved_searches_json on init_db. Fix: restore COLUMNS entries and call ensure_columns() before _rebuild_user_scoped_tables.
notes: Fixed in PR #42. Root cause: the user_id rebuild migration's INSERT SELECT references columns the COLUMNS forward-migration used to add — but that COLUMNS entry was deleted in the same commit. Fix moves ensure_columns() before _rebuild_user_scoped_tables and restores the COLUMNS entries.

title: WG-Gesucht filler blocked by #sec_advice modal on submit
description:
Reproduced 2026-05-05 against the live WG-Gesucht 'messenger' contact form (`form#messenger_form`). After filling the message, the submit click times out because a security-advice modal — `
…
` — is rendered above the page and intercepts pointer events. Playwright's auto-retry loop hits 58+ retries over 30s, then raises TimeoutError. Two bugs: 1. The filler (src/flatpilot/fillers/wg_gesucht.py) does not detect or dismiss `#sec_advice` before clicking `button[type=submit]`. The modal appears to fire on the first submit per session, possibly only for newly-logged-in accounts or accounts that haven't accepted the advice yet. 2. The filler does not catch playwright.TimeoutError from `.click()`. The raw TimeoutError bubbles past auto_apply._try_flat's `except FillError`, past run_pipeline_apply, and is caught only at pipeline.run_pipeline_once as a generic 'apply failed: TimeoutError'. Because no FillError was raised, apply.py:298 does not write a `status='failed'` row to applications — the failure is invisible in the audit trail. Worse, the bubble-out aborts the rest of the auto-apply queue: flats #34 and #46 in the same run were never attempted. Repro: - Fresh `flatpilot login wg-gesucht` (newly authenticated cookies) - `flatpilot apply ` against any active WG-Gesucht listing → submit click times out - applications table stays empty; apply_locks correctly released by `finally` Fix scope: - Add CONSENT-style selector tuple to dismiss `#sec_advice` before submit (probably `#sec_advice button:has-text('Verstanden')` or similar — verify in headed mode). - Wrap submit click in try/except PlaywrightTimeoutError → raise SubmitVerificationError(...) so the apply pipeline records a failed row and continues to the next candidate. Without (1) auto-apply is non-functional. Without (2) the failure mode is silent and corrupts queue ordering. Both should land together. Blocks the entire 'WG-Gesucht auto-apply works' claim — currently the only platform with a working filler at all.
close_reason: Fixed in branch fix/wg-gesucht-sec-advice-modal: pre-emptive #sec_advice dismissal + dismiss-and-retry on click timeout, with PlaywrightTimeoutError translated to SubmitVerificationError so apply pipeline records failed rows.

title: Add Kleinanzeigen login recipe
description:
`flatpilot login kleinanzeigen` raises UnknownPlatformError because src/flatpilot/login.py:_LOGIN_SITES only has a wg-gesucht entry. The Kleinanzeigen filler (src/flatpilot/fillers/kleinanzeigen.py) requires a logged-in cookie jar at ~/.flatpilot/sessions/kleinanzeigen/state.json — without a login recipe FlatPilot itself cannot seed those cookies, so every apply against Kleinanzeigen will surface as NotAuthenticatedError.

What's needed:
- Add a Kleinanzeigen entry to _LOGIN_SITES with login_url, warmup_url and consent_selectors (the latter two already exist in flatpilot.scrapers.kleinanzeigen).
- Manual smoke test: flatpilot login kleinanzeigen, log in by hand, verify state.json is written with non-session cookies and earliest expiry > 24h.

Blocks meaningful resolution of FlatPilot-92j (Kleinanzeigen selector verification needs an authenticated DOM, which needs cookies) and the Kleinanzeigen Connect button in FlatPilot-8jx.
close_reason: Login recipe registered in src/flatpilot/login.py:_LOGIN_SITES; cookie-seeding flow validated via flatpilot login kleinanzeigen, state.json written, follow-up dry-run picked up the cookies cleanly under FlatPilot-92j

title: Empirically verify Kleinanzeigen contact-form selectors
description: The Kleinanzeigen filler (FlatPilot-1o2) ships with provisional selectors transcribed from the unauthenticated DOM of a real Berlin listing on 2026-04-27 (form#viewad-contact-modal-form, button#viewad-contact-button-login-modal, textarea#viewad-contact-message, .ajaxform-success / .outcomebox-error). Authenticated DOM may differ — name / phone may be auto-prefilled, the trigger may behave differently for logged-in users, the modal may reveal differently. Resolution mirrors FlatPilot-fze: log into kleinanzeigen.de via polite_session in headed mode, navigate to a real listing's contact form, inspect the DOM, update SELECTORS in fillers/kleinanzeigen.py, re-run a dry-run until every field locates correctly. Blocks live submission to Kleinanzeigen with confidence.
close_reason: Selectors verified empirically against authenticated DOM 2026-05-05 on a live Berlin listing (Haffkrug 3-Zimmer, ad 3400774917). Authenticated UX uses the inline form#viewad-contact-form (visible by default), not the modal #viewad-contact-modal-form which is the unauth fallback. Updated SELECTORS to target the inline form; dry-run via KleinanzeigenFiller.fill(submit=False) succeeded — message field located, attachment-rejection still raises SelectorMissingError correctly.

title: dispatcher notifies matches from stale profile hashes
description:
notifications/dispatcher.dispatch_pending picks up any match row with decision='match' and a null notified_at, regardless of which profile_version_hash it was evaluated under. If the user changes their profile (e.g. tightens rent_max_warm via 'flatpilot init'), the dispatcher still sends matches that were valid under the OLD profile's rules and had never been notified. Symptom: user lowers rent cap, next 'flatpilot run' pings Telegram with listings that are over the new cap.

Fix: in dispatch_pending, compute the current profile_version_hash (sha256 of profile.model_dump_json, same logic as matcher.runner._profile_hash) and scope the pending-match query with 'WHERE profile_version_hash = ?'. Old-hash matches stay in the DB as historical records but never fire notifications again.

One-time cleanup on first run after the fix: mark any currently-pending-but-stale-hash matches as notified_at=now without actually sending (they're already orphans). Optional; in practice most users will have already drained them.

Discovered 2026-04-22 when the user's first real profile change (rent_max_warm 1800 -> 900) produced 34 over-limit Telegram pings from the old-hash match queue.
close_reason: Merged in PR #15 (dispatcher profile-hash scoping + stale-match suppression)

title: A7. Dockerfile + docker compose for local/scheduled runs
description:
Optional container path for running FlatPilot without installing Python 3.11+ and Playwright browsers on the host. Phase 5 will add more services (web UI, worker, DB), but for Phase 1 this is a single-service compose.yml that makes the CLI reproducible and schedulable.

Scope:
- Dockerfile based on the official Playwright Python image (Chromium pre-installed with system deps).
- Installs the flatpilot package via pip install -e '.[dev]' so code changes are picked up.
- docker-compose.yml with one service 'flatpilot'.
- Host volume mount for ~/.flatpilot so SQLite DB, sessions, and geocode cache persist across container restarts.
- .env support for TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN, SMTP creds, etc.
- .dockerignore to keep the build context small.
- README section documenting: docker compose run --rm flatpilot init, docker compose run --rm flatpilot run, etc.
- Local pip-install path stays working; Docker is an alternative, not a replacement.

If blocked, enumerate mitigations (lower cadence, longer cookie warm-up, residential IP, RSS-style email fallback) before committing to the scraper design.
notes: Probe run 2026-04-22, Berlin, city_id=8, interval=90s, duration=30min (20 polls), headless=true, Firefox UA, Docker container on arm64 macOS. title: Auto-apply: --drain loops until all reachable platforms hit cap, prints failure summary on exit
description:
Currently `flatpilot run --drain` exhausts the matched-flat queue and exits, often well below daily caps (user just saw 5/20 wg-gesucht submits in one run, then quit because the queue was empty).

Goal: a single invocation that scrape→match→apply repeatedly, sleeping cooldowns, until every "reachable" platform's daily cap is met (or 2 consecutive passes add zero new applyable flats). On any exit path — caps reached, empty-streak, SIGINT/SIGTERM — print a grouped, deduped summary of per-flat failures so the user can fix filler bugs.

Out of scope: changing `flatpilot run` (no flags) one-pass behavior, changing `--watch` semantics, file/Telegram summary delivery (terminal-only for now).

Design: `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-14-auto-apply-drain-loop-design.md`

title: auto-apply: stop spamming 'filler not registered' rows on every pass
description:
## Symptom

The applications table on the dev box has **310 rows** for inberlinwohnen of the form:

  status=failed  notes='auto_skipped: filler not registered for platform '\''inberlinwohnen'\'''

(observed 2026-05-14). Every \`run_pipeline_apply\` pass re-queries every matched flat, hits the \`completeness_ok\` → \`get_filler(platform)\` LookupError branch, and writes a fresh \`auto_skipped: filler not registered\` row. The exclusion clauses in the queue-selector SQL (\`auto_apply.py:188–218\`) cover submitted rows and recent listing_expired rows but NOT this case, so the spam grows unbounded.

## Why this is wrong

- 310 rows × N future passes = unbounded growth for a state that is permanent (until a filler is implemented). FlatPilot-6pq is filed as backlog with "probably not worth it" — the filler is not coming soon.
- Dashboard / status command have to filter past this noise on every render.
- Each pass burns a tiny amount of work (SQL row + console line) for zero diagnostic value past the first occurrence.

## Plan

In \`src/flatpilot/auto_apply.py::run_pipeline_apply\`, extend the queue-selector SQL with one more \`NOT EXISTS\` clause analogous to the listing_expired exclusion — but with no TTL (a missing filler is permanent until code lands):

\`\`\`sql
AND NOT EXISTS (
    SELECT 1 FROM applications a
    WHERE a.flat_id = m.flat_id
      AND a.user_id = ?
      AND a.method = 'auto'
      AND a.status = 'failed'
      AND a.notes LIKE 'auto_skipped: filler not registered%'
)
\`\`\`

Result: one row per flat per missing-filler platform (preserves first-time visibility); zero growth thereafter. If \`FlatPilot-6pq\` is ever picked up and a filler lands, a one-off cleanup query can delete those rows to re-enable the flats — out of scope here.

## Acceptance

1. New pipeline pass over the existing dataset does NOT add any new \`auto_skipped: filler not registered\` rows for inberlinwohnen flats that already have one.
2. New inberlinwohnen matches that have never been processed still get exactly one \`auto_skipped: filler not registered\` row (for visibility).
3. Unit / integration test in \`tests/test_run_pipeline_apply.py\` asserting: pre-existing filler-not-registered row → flat excluded from the queue scan.
4. Existing listing_expired exclusion test stays green.

## Out of scope

- Backfill cleanup of the 310 existing rows. They are already correctly classified as auto_skipped; if the dashboard becomes too cluttered, a one-shot delete query is a follow-up.
- Implementing the inberlinwohnen filler itself — that is FlatPilot-6pq (backlog).

## Related

- FlatPilot-6pq — inberlinwohnen / immoscout24 filler decision (backlog).
- FlatPilot-tgw — same exclusion pattern, for listing_expired (with TTL).
- FlatPilot-8kt — out-of-scope note mentioned this row spam as a separate bead candidate.

title: kleinanzeigen filler: surface HTTP status / server-error code on submit failure
description:
## Symptom (observed 2026-05-09)

Three identical failures on flat 590 (Terrassenwohnung Berlin-Dahlem) in `applications`:

  196 failed  kleinanzeigen: neither success nor error indicator appeared within 15000ms after submit ...
  171 failed  kleinanzeigen: neither success nor error indicator appeared within 7000ms after submit ...
  146 failed  ... same

Row 196 is *after* PR #52 bumped the success timeout from 7s to 15s, so the timeout bump alone did not resolve it.

## Screenshot evidence

Failure screenshot at ~/.flatpilot/screenshots/kleinanzeigen/3394499312-203-26318-20260509T222001.png shows a **full-page kleinanzeigen server-error page**:

  Fehler [400]
  Huch, da ist ein Fehler (400) aufgetreten

NOT inline-validation errors on un-filled structured fields (which was the hypothesis behind FlatPilot-ic1). The submit POST is reaching the kleinanzeigen server and being rejected with HTTP 400; the filler sees neither the success banner nor the inline error banner because the page has navigated away to a server-side error template.

## Why this matters

The existing `SubmitVerificationError` message ("neither success nor error indicator appeared") is misleading for this failure mode — it implies the page is silent, when in fact it is loudly showing a 400 page. That misleading signal pushed scoping toward FlatPilot-ic1 (Profile schema rewrite to fill 15 structured fields) as the cause; FlatPilot-ic1 may or may not be the right fix, but we cannot tell until we know whether the 400 is from missing form data, CSRF, anti-bot, or something else.

## Plan

Mirror the FlatPilot-8kt pattern: ship the diagnostic first, then commit to a fix from evidence.

In `flatpilot/fillers/kleinanzeigen.py::_verify_submitted`, after the success-marker timeout and before the catch-all "neither indicator" branch, detect the full-page server-error state and surface the status code:

1. Add a `server_error_marker` selector to `SELECTORS` that matches the error-page heading (text-based locator on `Fehler [\d+]`).
2. If the marker is visible, extract the bracketed status code (regex `Fehler \[(\d+)\]`) from the page text and raise `SubmitVerificationError` with a message that includes both the code and the form URL.
3. Failure screenshot continues to capture regardless (existing best-effort helper).

## Acceptance

1. When the post-submit page renders `Fehler [400]`, the filler raises `SubmitVerificationError` whose message includes `HTTP 400` (or `Fehler [400]`) and the contact URL — not the generic "neither success nor error indicator" message.
2. Unit test in `tests/test_filler_kleinanzeigen.py` covers the new branch: submit click → page text contains `Fehler [400]` → `SubmitVerificationError` with the status code in the exception message.
3. Existing branches (success banner; error banner; neither) remain green.
4. The reclassified failure note lets us decide between FlatPilot-ic1 and other root-cause fixes on the next live failure.

## Out of scope

- Capturing the HTTP response body from the submit POST via Playwright `page.on("response", ...)` — possible follow-up if the page-text marker proves unreliable.
- The actual fix for whatever causes the 400. That is FlatPilot-ic1 or a sibling bead, decided post-diagnostic.

## Related

- FlatPilot-ic1 — premised on missing-required-fields; needs this diagnostic to confirm or refute.
- FlatPilot-8kt — same diagnostic-first pattern.

title: wg-gesucht filler: capture failure screenshots + investigate silent submit rejection
description:
## Symptom (observed 2026-05-09)

Auto-apply submitted flat 20 (wg-gesucht) successfully but failed on flat 29 (id=12069885, 'Apartment in Berlin-Mitte, short term stay May 11 – 28') with:

  wg-gesucht: submit did not navigate away from the form URL
    (https://www.wg-gesucht.de/nachricht-senden/wohnungen-in-Berlin-Mitte.12069885.html)
    — message likely rejected by validation

Log shows the pre-emptive #sec_advice modal dismissal fired immediately before submit (selector hit was `#sec_advice [data-dismiss='modal']`, the third-priority Bootstrap close-X — NOT the preferred `Verstanden` button).

## Verification done in headed Chromium (2026-05-09)

Manually opened the same form URL in a headed Playwright session reusing ~/.flatpilot/sessions/wg-gesucht/state.json. Filled the form by hand and clicked submit. WG-Gesucht navigated to:

  https://www.wg-gesucht.de/nachricht.html?nachrichten-id=587188534

Page also surfaced two `.alert-warning` banners about an active ad-blocker. So:

- **The filler's success-detection contract is correct** (real submits redirect away from /nachricht-senden/).
- **The bot's submit silently does nothing** — page stays on form URL with no banner emitted.

## Candidate root causes

1. The pre-emptively-dismissed modal was NOT #sec_advice but a co-existing Bootstrap modal (e.g. ad-blocker warning) sharing `[data-dismiss='modal']`. Closing it was harmless; the real submit failure is unobserved.
2. #sec_advice is now a real submit-gate where only the `Verstanden` button counts as acknowledgement; clicking the X cancels the submit.
3. WG-Gesucht's anti-bot detected the Playwright fingerprint and silently swallowed the submit (no banner, no navigation).

## Plan

**Step 1 (this bead, first PR — small):** Capture failure screenshots from the wg-gesucht filler whenever SubmitVerificationError is about to be raised. Write to ~/.flatpilot/screenshots/<platform>/<flat-slug>-<isots>.png. Best-effort — a screenshot failure must never mask the original error. Future failures become self-diagnosing.

**Step 2 (this bead, follow-up):** Reproduce the failure on a fresh flat (the next non-cooldown match), inspect the screenshot, decide between root-cause hypotheses 1/2/3 and apply the targeted fix:
- If hypothesis 1: tighten advisory selectors so they only match #sec_advice itself, not other modals.
- If hypothesis 2: stop trying to dismiss #sec_advice; instead click `Verstanden` only, and if it's not visible, leave the modal alone and try clicking submit again (the modal may auto-dismiss on form submission).
- If hypothesis 3: investigate Playwright stealth options or accept the failure rate.

## Out of scope

- inberlinwohnen 'filler not registered' auto_skipped row spam (separate bead candidate).
- Notification routing (Telegram + SMTP not currently enabled in user's profile).
notes: Closed 2026-05-14: all three steps shipped pre-bead-audit. Step 1 (failure screenshot) PR #48 / commit 6526b34. Step 3 (in-place success marker via SUBMIT_SUCCESS_INDICATORS + _verify_submitted) PR #49 / commit 9ce3939. Backfill maintenance command 'flatpilot reclassify-submits' PR #50 / commit c5f50a8. Test coverage at tests/test_filler_submit.py:200/213/431/458 — all 16 filler-submit tests green.

title: wizard: preview Anschreiben at end of init and offer to customize
description:
Currently flatpilot init writes the profile and exits without showing the user what will actually be sent to landlords. Users (myself included) discover too late that ~/.flatpilot/templates/<platform>.md doesn't exist or that the shipped example contains German boilerplate they didn't review (SCHUFA promise, no signature name, English 'employed' literal in employment_status placeholder).

Proposal: at the end of the wizard, after the saved-searches menu and before the final 'Save profile?' confirm:

1. For each platform listed in any saved_search.platforms, check if ~/.flatpilot/templates/<platform>.md exists.
2. If missing, render the shipped anschreiben.example.md against the just-built profile + a synthetic placeholder flat (title='Beispielwohnung 2 Zi', district='Mitte', rent_warm_eur=750, listing_url='https://example/123', rooms=2) and print the rendered output via rich.
3. Ask: 'Use this default Anschreiben? [y/n]'.
   - 'y': copy the shipped example into ~/.flatpilot/templates/<platform>.md.
   - 'n': open $EDITOR (fallback: instruct the user to create the file manually at the printed path); do not start auto-apply until file exists.
4. If template already exists, render it the same way and ask 'Keep current Anschreiben? [y/n]' so the user is reminded what they're about to send each production run.

Acceptance:
- New user running 'flatpilot init' for the first time sees the rendered example before it's written.
- Returning user re-running init sees their existing template rendered with current profile values.
- 'flatpilot doctor' should additionally check for missing templates per saved-search platform (currently it doesn't — that's the channel the user actually hit this through).
- Tests cover: (a) shipped example renders without TemplateSubstitutionError against the example profile, (b) wizard skips template prompt for platforms not used by any saved_search, (c) accepting the default writes the file, declining doesn't.

Related: composer is already at flatpilot/compose.py with placeholder substitution; the example lives at src/flatpilot/anschreiben.example.md (resources.files). Doctor's saved-searches check is at doctor.py:144 — same place to add a per-platform template check.

title: Per-flat concurrency lock in apply_to_flat to prevent double-submit
description:
apply.py:apply_to_flat reads 'SELECT id FROM applications WHERE flat_id=? AND status=submitted' before inserting, but there's no lock between the read and the write. Two near-simultaneous dashboard apply POSTs for the same flat — or a double-click on the dashboard Apply button — can both pass the existence check before either INSERTs, causing a double submission to the landlord.

Resolution: serialize per-flat. The dashboard runs in a single Python process (BaseHTTPServer + spawned 'flatpilot apply' subprocesses), so a process-wide threading.Lock dict keyed by flat_id is sufficient for the in-process race. The cross-process race (CLI 'flatpilot apply N' running while a dashboard subprocess is also applying to N) is rarer but can be guarded with a SQLite UNIQUE partial index or an advisory lock row. Pick the simplest primitive that closes the in-process race; document the cross-process gap if not also fixed.

Acceptance: a TDD test that races two threads calling apply_to_flat for the same flat_id asserts only one applications row with status='submitted' is created (the loser raises AlreadyAppliedError or equivalent).

Carry-over flagged during PR #24/#25 review.

title: Add subprocess timeout to server._spawn_apply
description:
The dashboard server's apply handler shells out to 'flatpilot apply <id>' via subprocess.run() in server.py:63 with no timeout. Because the dashboard uses BaseHTTPServer (single-threaded), a hung Playwright in the child process freezes the entire server until killed.

Resolution: pass a sensible timeout= to subprocess.run (long enough for a real headed apply, short enough to bound dashboard hang — e.g. 120s). On TimeoutExpired, return a structured error result {ok: False, returncode: None, stdout_tail: '<timed out after Ns>'} so the browser surfaces a clean message instead of a stuck spinner.

Acceptance: a subprocess.run patched to sleep past the timeout returns the structured timeout result and the server thread is freed; existing tests still pass.

Carry-over flagged during PR #24/#25 review.

title: Kleinanzeigen Apply filler
description:
Implement the per-platform Apply filler for Kleinanzeigen so 'flatpilot apply <flat_id>' works on Kleinanzeigen listings.

Current state (2026-04-27): the fillers registry only contains 'wg-gesucht' (src/flatpilot/fillers/wg_gesucht.py). 'flatpilot apply' on a Kleinanzeigen flat fails with KeyError: 'No filler registered for platform kleinanzeigen'. Discovered while smoke-testing PR #25; previously un-tracked.

Implementation: add src/flatpilot/fillers/kleinanzeigen.py modelled on the existing WG-Gesucht filler. The contact form on Kleinanzeigen detail pages (https://www.kleinanzeigen.de/s-anzeige/.../<id>) is reached via the 'Nachricht senden' panel on the right; needs a logged-in cookie (already captured in ~/.flatpilot/sessions/kleinanzeigen/state.json from the scraper login flow).

Scope:
- Reach detail page from listing_url, dismiss any consent banner reuse from kleinanzeigen scraper module.
- Locate name / email / message fields, file-upload widget, submit button.
- Fill via the existing FillContext (profile name, email, attachments, rendered anschreiben).
- Honor --dry-run: fill but do NOT click submit.
- Anti-bot: reuse SessionConfig with stealth=True (kleinanzeigen scraper already uses it).
- Tests: tests/test_filler_kleinanzeigen.py mirroring tests/test_filler_submit.py — fixture HTML for the form, mocked Playwright, captured submit/dry-run pathways.

Acceptance: 'flatpilot apply <flat_id>' for a logged-in Kleinanzeigen profile fills the form (--dry-run shows preview, no submit), and a non-dry-run successfully posts a contact message and writes status='submitted' to the applications table.

title: L-foundations: `flatpilot login <platform>` CLI for cookie seeding
description:
Headed-browser CLI command that lets the user log in to a rental platform once so the resulting cookies persist to ~/.flatpilot/sessions/<platform>/state.json and every subsequent polite_session(headless=True) call (scrape, dry-run filler, future apply) reuses them.

UX: `flatpilot login wg-gesucht` opens a Playwright Chromium window pointed at the platform's login URL, prints "Log in by hand (including any 2FA / captcha), then press Enter here when you see your dashboard.", waits on stdin, captures storage_state on Enter, closes the window, prints "saved session for <platform> (cookies expire ~<date>)".

Implementation: ~30-50 LOC. Reuses polite_session() with headless=False overridden via a new SessionConfig field or dedicated helper. Per-platform login URL constants live in each platform module (scrapers/wg_gesucht.py, future kleinanzeigen.py etc.) — no per-platform login flow code is needed because the user drives the browser themselves.

Why no credential storage: passwords leaking from ~/.flatpilot would compromise the user's accounts permanently and are commonly reused across services; the Docker runtime can't reach the host OS keyring; programmatic login is a strong anti-bot signal that risks getting accounts flagged. Cookies-only is the safe default. (Discussed in PR #18 hand-off.)

Reuses across every platform we'll ever add (J/K/I/N epics) — one command, all platforms.

Acceptance: `flatpilot login wg-gesucht` opens a window, waits for the user, saves cookies that the L3 dry-run filler can then reuse without raising NotAuthenticatedError.

title: Empirically verify WG-Gesucht contact-form selectors
description: The L3 dry-run filler (FlatPilot-4zkt) ships with provisional CSS selectors for the WG-Gesucht contact form (see fillers/wg_gesucht.py SELECTORS dict). They were chosen from documented form names but have not been validated against the live form, which requires an authenticated session and a real listing URL. First real dry-run will surface mismatches as SelectorMissingError. Resolution: log into wg-gesucht.de via the polite_session (headed mode), navigate to a real listing's contact form, inspect the DOM, update the SELECTORS constants, and re-run the dry-run until every field locates correctly. Blocks L4 because the apply CLI must produce reliable previews before exposing a submit path.
close_reason: Merged in PR #19; selectors verified against live messenger form on two Berlin listings

title: CLI commands don't load ~/.flatpilot/.env (only doctor does)
description:
config.load_env() is only called from the doctor command. notify, run, and scrape never call it, so a user running FlatPilot without Docker will find that TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN / SMTP_* in ~/.flatpilot/.env are ignored — they'd have to export the vars in their shell. With Docker this works because docker-compose.yml's env_file pushes the repo .env into the container, but that's a compose-specific workaround.

Fix: call config.load_env() in the _bootstrap typer callback in src/flatpilot/cli.py so every command loads the app-dir .env at startup, regardless of invocation path.

Minor follow-up to the F-epic; noticed while wiring up the first Telegram notification on 2026-04-22.

Kleinanzeigen anti-bot D0 probe (2026-04-22)
--------------------------------------------
Result: 158 / 158 polls = 100% ok over ~4 h at 90 s cadence. Zero
Cloudflare challenges (Turnstile / 'Just a moment' interstitial), zero

title: kleinanzeigen filler: fill landlord-required structured fields (Anrede, Vorname, etc.)
description:
The kleinanzeigen contact form has up to 15 fields (Anrede, Vorname, Nachname, Telefon, Straße/Nr., PLZ, SCHUFA-Auskunft, Haushaltstyp, Arbeitsverhältnis, …). Each landlord configures which fields they want filled. The filler currently fills only the message; on listings that mark structured fields as required, the submit either silently ignores us or fails inline validation in a way that fires neither the success nor the error banner.

Symptom: 'kleinanzeigen: neither success nor error indicator appeared within 7000ms after submit' (now 15s, see FlatPilot-8kt's followup PR). Observed on flat 590 'Terrassenwohnung Berlin-Dahlem'. Sister flat 20 succeeded with the same filler — different landlords request different fields.

## Plan

1. Extend Profile schema with the kleinanzeigen-specific fields:
   - anrede: Literal['frau', 'herr', 'divers', 'keine_angabe'] (or use existing 'gender' if added)
   - given_name, surname (split from a single 'name' OR use 'household_contact_name')
   - phone (optional)
   - address: street, plz, city (already have city; add street + plz)
   - schufa_status: enum
   - household_type: enum
   - employment_status (already have 'status', map kleinanzeigen-specific labels to it)

2. Wizard: prompt for these on `flatpilot init` (and `flatpilot init --reset`).

3. Filler: after _reveal_contact_form, iterate visible fields under form#viewad-contact-form and fill each from the profile if a match exists. Use Playwright's locator(label='Anrede') / select_option for dropdowns. Skip optional fields the profile doesn't define.

4. Tests: fixture HTML for the 15-field variant; fixture HTML for the message-only variant; assert each is filled correctly.

## Out of scope

- wg-gesucht does not use structured fields — this is kleinanzeigen-only for now.
- inberlinwohnen has no filler at all (FlatPilot-mrt — separate decision).

## Why deferred

The followup PR after FlatPilot-8kt adds failure-screenshot capture + bumps the success-detection timeout to 15s. That gives diagnostic reach AND fixes the failure case where kleinanzeigen XHR was slow but the submit DID complete. If the timeout fix alone makes flat 590-style failures go away, this bead drops priority. If failures persist with the longer timeout AND the screenshot shows visible inline-validation errors on un-filled fields, this becomes the next blocking work.

title: wg-gesucht filler: classify 'no contact CTA' as listing_expired (already-messaged listings)
description:
WG-Gesucht hides the 'Nachricht senden' contact CTA on a listing once the logged-in user has messaged it. The wg-gesucht filler currently raises a non-classified FormNotFoundError ('no contact CTA matching ...') in that case, which apply_to_flat records as status='failed' rather than status='failed' WITH notes='auto_skipped: listing_expired'.

Effect:
- The flat keeps getting retried by run_pipeline_apply on every pass until max_failures_per_flat (default 3) blocks it. Each retry costs a Playwright session, a polite_session ramp-up, and a row in the applications table — pure waste.
- max_failures_per_flat is meant for genuine filler regressions (selector breakage, auth loops); using it to gate already-applied listings is a misuse that hides real failures.
- Cooldown still fires after each 'failure', wasting cap headroom by spacing out retries that will never succeed.

## Plan

In flatpilot/fillers/wg_gesucht.py, _reveal_contact_form (or wherever 'no contact CTA' is raised), wrap the FormNotFoundError raise so callers can detect 'CTA missing on a logged-in session' as ListingExpiredError instead. The existing FlatPilot-tgw machinery then:
- Writes notes='auto_skipped: listing_expired (...)'
- Excludes the flat from the apply queue for LISTING_EXPIRED_TTL (7 days)
- Doesn't count toward max_failures_per_flat

## Acceptance

1. WGGesuchtFiller raises ListingExpiredError when the listing page renders without a 'Nachricht senden' CTA AND the page does not show the login wall (LOGIN_URL_FRAGMENTS guard already handles that case).
2. Unit test in tests/test_filler_submit.py covers both branches: CTA missing + logged in → ListingExpiredError; CTA missing + login wall → NotAuthenticatedError (current behaviour).
3. Pipeline integration test verifies the 'auto_skipped: listing_expired' note is written and the flat is excluded from subsequent queue scans for 7 days.

## Related
- FlatPilot-8kt — the success-detection fix exposes this gap because real successes now correctly mark flats submitted=True; legacy false-fail rows from the pre-fix era keep poisoning the queue, so the 'auto-apply only finds dead listings' symptom is most visible right now.
- FlatPilot-tgw — original listing-expired classification + TTL.
notes: Closed 2026-05-14: already implemented by FlatPilot-tgw (commit 3fd5f08). src/flatpilot/fillers/wg_gesucht.py:267 raises ListingExpiredError when contact CTA is absent; unit test tests/test_filler_submit.py:363 covers the logged-in branch. The acceptance's 'CTA missing + login wall' sub-branch is structurally unreachable — listing URLs don't redirect to /login for logged-out users; only the contact-form URL does, caught by the second _guard_login() at wg_gesucht.py:279 before any CTA check runs.

title: flatpilot status: surface auto-apply runtime state (pause, caps, cooldowns)
description:
flatpilot status today reports DB counts and last-run info. It does not surface the runtime state of the auto-apply subsystem, so a user has no quick way to answer:

- Is auto-apply paused? (~/.flatpilot/PAUSE present?)
- How many of today's daily caps are used per platform? (daily_cap_per_platform vs. submitted today)
- Is a per-platform cooldown active right now, and how many seconds remain?
- How many flats are currently above max_failures_per_flat and being skipped?

All of these are already computable from helpers in flatpilot/auto_apply.py (is_paused, daily_cap_remaining, cooldown_remaining_sec, failures_for_flat) and the applications table — flatpilot status just doesn't display them.

## Proposed surface

Extend the `flatpilot status` Rich output with a new "Auto-apply" panel:

  Auto-apply
    State:           ACTIVE              (or "PAUSED — ~/.flatpilot/PAUSE")
    wg-gesucht       12 / 20 today · cooldown 0s
    kleinanzeigen     3 / 20 today · cooldown 47s remaining
    inberlinwohnen    — (no filler registered)
    Flats over max_failures: 2

## Acceptance

1. `flatpilot status` shows the panel above with live values pulled from the same helpers used by the pipeline.
2. PAUSED state is visually distinct (yellow) so it cannot be missed.
3. Platforms without a registered filler are listed but marked as not auto-applying.
4. New unit test in tests/ covers the panel rendering with a populated DB fixture (paused vs. active, cap exhausted, cooldown active, max-failure flats).

## Out of scope

- No new auto-apply behavior; this is a read-only display.
- No web UI surface — CLI only (UI work is paused, see project notes).

## Why now

Phase 4 (FlatPilot-f2fa) shipped the engine; without this panel the user has to read SQLite to understand what the engine is currently doing. Closing the observability gap is a natural follow-up.
close_reason: Added Auto-apply panel to flatpilot status (cli.py _print_auto_apply_panel) showing pause state (ACTIVE/PAUSED), per-platform usage and cooldown countdown, platforms-without-filler marker, and flats-over-max-failures count. New flats_over_max_failures helper in auto_apply.py with unit tests. 9 new tests in test_auto_apply_status_panel.py; full suite 505/505 green.

title: Add ImmoScout24 login recipe (or formally drop)
description:
FlatPilot-8jx's UI mockup shows an 'ImmoScout24 [Connect →]' button on the Connected Accounts page, but src/flatpilot/login.py:_LOGIN_SITES has no immoscout24 entry — the underlying CLI cannot drive that flow. Today the ImmoScout24 integration is RSS-only (flatpilot.scrapers.immoscout24_rss reads URLs the user pastes from their account); there is no filler and arguably no need to seed cookies.

Two ways to resolve:
1. Add a login recipe pointing at https://www.immobilienscout24.de/anmelden.html so the Connect button has a backend, even though no current code path consumes the cookies. Keeps the UI promise consistent.
2. Decide RSS-only is the supported integration, drop the ImmoScout24 button from FlatPilot-8jx's scope, and close this as won't-do.

Resolution should pick one of those, not leave the contradiction.

Blocks the ImmoScout24 part of FlatPilot-8jx.
close_reason: Added login recipe for ImmoScout24 (anmelden.html) so 'flatpilot login immoscout24' and the Web UI Connect button (FlatPilot-8jx) have a backend, even though no current code path consumes the seeded cookies. Branch: feat/immoscout24-login-recipe.

title: N-strikes-out backoff after repeated FillError on the same flat
description: run_pipeline_apply (in flatpilot/auto_apply.py) currently retries failed auto-applies on every pass. A FillError that's persistent (e.g. session expired loop, selector regression on a single listing) generates a status='failed' applications row each pass without any throttle — apply_locks only prevents PARALLEL attempts on the same flat, not serial retries across passes. With 50 fresh matches in one scrape, this hammers the platform. Solution: count status='failed' AND method='auto' AND triggered_by_saved_search=ss_name AND notes NOT LIKE 'auto_skipped:%' rows for a given flat_id; after N (default 3) skip the flat and write one row noting persistent-failure backoff. Surfaces in dashboard via existing notes display. Reviewer flagged this in the FlatPilot-f2fa code review.
close_reason: Implemented in commit ad63d26 on docs/auto-apply-safety-rails-spec branch (PR #32). 308/308 tests pass.

title: log.py: --verbose flag should surface DEBUG logs on console, not only in log file
description:
setup_logging(level) is called with level=logging.DEBUG when the user passes --verbose, but console_handler.setLevel is hardcoded to logging.INFO regardless of the level argument (log.py line ~49). This means flatpilot --verbose writes DEBUG entries to ~/.flatpilot/logs/flatpilot.log but the terminal still shows only INFO — contradicting the --verbose help text ('Enable DEBUG logging'). A user who adds --verbose to diagnose a scraper issue sees no extra output on screen.

Scope: change console_handler.setLevel(logging.INFO) to console_handler.setLevel(level) in log.py. One-line fix.

Acceptance: flatpilot --verbose doctor prints DEBUG-level log lines to stderr; flatpilot doctor (no flag) shows only INFO and above; pytest 233→233.

title: tests: consolidate _FakeCtxMgr/_FakePageCtxMgr duplicates in scraper tests
description:
test_inberlinwohnen_scraper.py defines _FakeCtxMgr and _FakePageCtxMgr three separate times (lines ~168/178, ~231/241, ~285/295). test_kleinanzeigen_scraper.py defines its own pair (lines ~112/122). Each pair wraps a slightly different HTML response but the boilerplate context-manager structure (class _FakeCtxMgr / __enter__ / __exit__ nesting a _FakePageCtxMgr) is identical boilerplate repeated 4+ times. A factory function or parametrizable helper in tests/conftest.py (or a new tests/scraper_fakes.py) would reduce this.

Scope: extract a make_session_fakes(html_by_url) factory into tests/conftest.py or a new tests/scraper_fakes.py. Replace the 3 duplicate pairs in test_inberlinwohnen_scraper.py and the pair in test_kleinanzeigen_scraper.py with factory calls. Note: imports inside test function bodies must remain in-function for monkeypatch isolation; only the class definitions move. Expected diff: ~80 LOC removed, ~25 LOC added, net -55 LOC across 2-3 test files.

Acceptance: pytest 233→233; test_inberlinwohnen_scraper.py contains at most one class named _FakeCtxMgr.

title: pyproject.toml: install mypy and configure [tool.mypy] for the project
description:
pyproject.toml has no [tool.mypy] section, mypy is not in the dev extras, and python -m mypy exits with 'No module named mypy'. The project uses Protocol, TypedDict, Literal, pydantic BaseModel, and explicit return-type annotations throughout — but none of it is machine-verified. A codebase that annotates its types without a type checker in CI is exercising theatre, not safety: a bad Any leaking into a Protocol method or a wrong TypedDict key silently passes all existing tests.

Scope: add mypy>=1.10 to [project.optional-dependencies] dev in pyproject.toml. Add a [tool.mypy] section with python_version='3.11', ignore_missing_imports=true (Playwright stubs are absent), strict=false as a baseline. Run mypy src/flatpilot/ and fix the errors OR add per-module ignores with # type: ignore[...] with a documented reason for each. Target: mypy exits 0 from a fresh venv. Expected effort: 1-2h of annotation cleanup and suppression tagging.

Acceptance: .venv/bin/mypy src/flatpilot/ exits 0 (or with a documented error count <= N if some suppression is accepted); ruff passes; pytest 233→233.

title: errors.py: add FlatPilotError base class to unify project exception hierarchy
description:
Project exceptions have no common parent. ProfileMissingError(RuntimeError) is in errors.py, AlreadyAppliedError(RuntimeError) in apply.py, UnknownPlatformError(ValueError) and ContainerDetectedError(RuntimeError) in login.py, AttachmentError in attachments.py, TemplateError in compose.py — and FillError(RuntimeError) in fillers/base.py already anchors a well-structured sub-hierarchy. Without a shared base a caller who wants to catch 'any flatpilot-level error' must enumerate every class. A common FlatPilotError(Exception) base in errors.py lets callers write except FlatPilotError and lets mypy verify it.

Scope: add class FlatPilotError(Exception) to errors.py. Make ProfileMissingError, UnknownCityError, AlreadyAppliedError, UnknownPlatformError, ContainerDetectedError, AttachmentError, TemplateError, and FillError all inherit from FlatPilotError (FillError is in fillers/base.py which can import errors.py cleanly). Add FlatPilotError to errors.__all__. Verify CLI exception handlers still fire correctly. ~20 LOC across 6 files.

Acceptance: isinstance(ProfileMissingError(), FlatPilotError) is True for every project exception class; ruff passes; pytest 233→233.

title: cli.py: deduplicate scraper registration side-effect imports
description:
The three # noqa: F401 — triggers @register lines for inberlinwohnen, kleinanzeigen, and wg_gesucht appear in two separate functions inside cli.py: _run_pipeline_scrape (lines 216-218) and the scrape() command body (lines 276-278). Adding a fourth scraper requires edits in two places, which is easy to miss and will cause 'no scrapers registered' bugs under flatpilot run but not flatpilot scrape (or vice versa). Extract a _ensure_scrapers_registered() helper called from both sites — three lines of imports, called twice.

Scope: add a 5-line _ensure_scrapers_registered() function to cli.py (or to pipeline.py after FlatPilot-we9 lands), replace both duplicate blocks with a single call. 2 files, ~5 LOC change.

Acceptance: ruff passes, pytest 233→233. Adding a mock 4th scraper import requires exactly one new line.

title: cli.py: extract pipeline orchestration helpers into flatpilot/pipeline.py
description:
cli.py is 663 LOC and contains six private orchestration helpers (_run_pipeline_once, _run_pipeline_scrape, _run_pipeline_match, _run_pipeline_notify, _run_scrape_pass, _insert_flat) that belong in a pipeline layer, not in the CLI presentation layer. CLAUDE.md already lists pipeline.py in the architecture overview but the file does not exist. The cross-layer smell is clearest in _insert_flat, which does a database INSERT and then calls flatpilot.matcher.dedup.assign_canonical — a CLI module importing the matcher module is a layering inversion. Moving these helpers to flatpilot/pipeline.py shrinks cli.py to ~500 LOC, gives the functions module-level imports instead of function-local ones, and makes the architecture match the documented design.

Scope: create src/flatpilot/pipeline.py containing the six helpers; update cli.py to import from pipeline; update any test callsites (search for _run_pipeline or _run_scrape_pass). Expected diff ~150 LOC moved, net-new 0 LOC of logic.

Acceptance: ruff check passes, pytest 233→233, flatpilot --help completes in <100ms, cli.py is <520 LOC.

title: Close cross-process Apply race (CLI vs dashboard) via apply_locks table
description:
The server-side threading.Lock added in FlatPilot-cy9 (PR #N) closes the dashboard double-click race but not the cross-process race: a user typing 'flatpilot apply 42' at a terminal while the dashboard ALSO fires Apply for flat 42 will still race. Both processes pass apply_to_flat's status='submitted' SELECT/INSERT check before either INSERTs, sending two messages to the landlord.

Resolution: an apply_locks table (or partial unique index on applications(flat_id) WHERE status='submitted') with stale-row cleanup. Add a CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS apply_locks (flat_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, acquired_at TEXT NOT NULL, pid INTEGER NOT NULL); INSERT-or-fail in apply_to_flat before filler.fill(); DELETE in finally. Stale rows older than e.g. 30min should be treated as cleanable on next attempt to handle process crashes.

Acceptance: a TDD test races two subprocess.run calls of 'flatpilot apply N' (or two separate Python processes) for the same flat_id; only one applications row with status='submitted' exists afterwards, the loser raises AlreadyAppliedError equivalent.

Out of scope for FlatPilot-cy9 because it would require a schema change with stale-row handling and grew the PR beyond its theme. The in-process race (the actual user-visible dashboard double-click bug) is closed; this is the residual hardening.

title: Move init_db() out of per-request server handlers to startup
description:
server.py calls init_db() inside _handle_skip (line 154) and _handle_response (line 175), so the DB is initialized on every POST request that mutates state. database.init_db is idempotent and reasonably cheap, but per-request init is still wasted work and obscures the lifecycle.

Resolution: call init_db() once inside serve() (the entry point that creates the HTTPServer), before HTTPServer.serve_forever(). Remove the per-handler calls. As a secondary benefit, this makes the server fail fast at startup if the SQLite file can't be opened, instead of failing on the first user click.

Acceptance: grep init_db() in server.py shows one call site (inside serve()); existing tests still pass; a deliberately broken DB path makes 'flatpilot dashboard' raise during startup rather than during the first POST.

Carry-over flagged during PR #24/#25 review.

title: Hoist ProfileMissingError and UnknownCityError into flatpilot.errors
description:
Two duplicated/misplaced exceptions across the codebase:

1. UnknownCityError is defined verbatim in scrapers/wg_gesucht.py:96 and scrapers/kleinanzeigen.py:89. Future scrapers (Phase 4 ImmoScout RSS) will need the same class. Keep one definition.

2. ProfileMissingError lives in apply.py:46 but server._handle_skip / _handle_response do their own ad-hoc 'load_profile() is None' checks rather than catching a shared exception. Same intent, two paths — drift risk.

Resolution: create flatpilot/errors.py with both classes (and document it as the home for cross-module exceptions). Re-export from existing modules for one release cycle if needed for any external imports — but inside the package, all callers should import from flatpilot.errors directly.

Acceptance: 'grep class UnknownCityError' returns one definition; server.py and apply.py both import ProfileMissingError from flatpilot.errors; existing tests still pass.

Carry-over flagged during PR #24/#25 review.

title: Allowlist read-only bd / pytest / ruff in .claude/settings.json
description: Add a permissions.allow block to .claude/settings.json covering the read-only bd queries (show, list, ready, stats, search, memories, export) and the standard test / lint commands (pytest -q/-v/tests/*, ruff check src tests) that every Phase 3 task uses. Reduces permission prompt friction without granting any state-mutating capability — bd update / bd close / bd remember / bd forget are deliberately not allowlisted, nor is anything that writes to disk or hits the network. Aligns with the project's fewer-permission-prompts skill recommendation.

title: Paginate inberlinwohnen.de Wohnungsfinder beyond page 1
description:
The Phase 1 inberlinwohnen scraper (FlatPilot-rqks) reads only page 1 of https://inberlinwohnen.de/wohnungsfinder/ — ~10 listings per pass. The full listing today is ~216 entries across 22 numbered pages. This is fine for steady-state polling (new listings surface on page 1) but on first install we miss the older inventory.

Implementation: identify the pagination URL pattern (server-rendered or Livewire-update? probe with curl or a manual scrape), extend fetch_new to walk pages until either (a) all hits already exist in the flats table or (b) a configurable cap (e.g. 5 pages) is reached. Respect a per-page polite delay; the existing polite_session covers per-fetch headers.

Acceptance: a single 'flatpilot scrape --platform inberlinwohnen' run on a fresh install ingests >100 listings (assuming the page count is ~22 today), and steady-state polling still costs only ~1 page per pass.

title: doctor: per-platform cookie-expiry check
description:
Add a doctor check per registered platform that probes a known logged-in-only URL and classifies the response as OK / EXPIRED / NEVER_LOGGED_IN. Surfaces "your wg-gesucht session expires in N days, run `flatpilot login wg-gesucht`" at `flatpilot doctor` time so users discover expiry proactively, not at the moment an apply attempt blows up at midnight.

Implementation: per-platform constant for the canary URL (e.g. WG-Gesucht's /mein-wg-gesucht.html). The check uses polite_session() in headless mode, navigates, and checks for a login redirect (existing LOGIN_URL_FRAGMENTS pattern from fillers/wg_gesucht.py) plus an optional "logged in" DOM marker. Cookie expiry timestamps come from the storage_state JSON.

Status mapping into the existing doctor table:
- OK -> green, 'session valid until <date>'
- EXPIRED -> yellow optional, 'run `flatpilot login <platform>`'
- NEVER_LOGGED_IN -> yellow optional, 'no session yet — run `flatpilot login <platform>` if you want to apply via this platform'

Acceptance: `flatpilot doctor` shows a row per registered platform with its session status; expiry warnings fire at least 3 days before the actual cookies expire.

title: doctor's Telegram check looks for TELEGRAM_CHAT_ID in env, but chat_id lives in profile.json
description:
doctor.py:_check_telegram reports 'optional / missing: TELEGRAM_CHAT_ID' when TELEGRAM_CHAT_ID is not an environment variable. But FlatPilot's design stores chat_id in profile.json (notifications.telegram.chat_id) — only the bot token is env-based (via profile.notifications.telegram.bot_token_env, default TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN). The check is therefore misleading: a user with a correctly configured profile still sees 'missing chat_id'.

Fix: rewrite _check_telegram to:
 1. Try to load_profile(); if no profile, report 'profile not set — run init'.
 2. If profile.notifications.telegram.enabled, check os.environ for profile.notifications.telegram.bot_token_env and for profile.notifications.telegram.chat_id being non-empty.
 3. If not enabled, report 'disabled in profile' (no missing-var nag).
Similarly for _check_smtp: only warn about SMTP_* when profile.notifications.email.enabled.

Noticed 2026-04-22 while onboarding the user's real profile post-Phase-1 merge. Purely cosmetic — no functional bug.

title: Add auto-apply fillers for inberlinwohnen + immoscout24 (currently scrape+notify only)
description:
Reverses the decision recorded in FlatPilot-mrt. Today, FlatPilot scrapes and notifies on inberlinwohnen.de and ImmoScout24 but does not auto-apply: the registry has no filler for those platforms, the auto path silently skips them, and `flatpilot apply <id>` exits with a friendly "auto-apply isn't supported on <platform> — apply manually" message (UnsupportedPlatformError).

This bead is filed as backlog/blocked because the team's current call (FlatPilot-mrt) is that supporting these is *probably not worth it*. Reopen and unblock only if the trade-offs change. Keep this bead alive — don't delete — so the rationale remains discoverable.

## Why it's hard

### inberlinwohnen.de
- Listings on the Wohnungsfinder feed deeplink off-site to each landlord's expose page (degewo, Howoge, Gesobau, Stadt und Land, WBM, GeWoSüd, …).
- There is no single contact form. A working filler means writing one filler per operator (~6+ for full coverage), each with its own selectors, login flow, captcha shape, and breakage cadence.
- Estimated maintenance: at least one operator's form changes per quarter. Each break blocks any user trying to apply on that operator until we patch.

### ImmoScout24
- FlatPilot intentionally consumes ImmoScout24 via RSS only (FlatPilot-l9hm, FlatPilot-h7q). The HTML scraper was retired because it broke too often and triggered anti-bot blocks.
- Adding a filler re-introduces the HTML/Playwright path the RSS migration removed. Same fragility, same anti-bot risk.
- ImmoScout24 also charges premium accounts to *contact* listings — many listings are not freely contactable even after login.

## Acceptance (if this bead is ever picked up)

Decompose into per-operator beads, not one big PR:
- `feat: degewo filler` (highest-volume operator)
- `feat: Howoge filler`
- `feat: Gesobau filler`
- `feat: <next operator>` …
- `feat: ImmoScout24 filler` (separate; needs its own scraper revival or premium-account flow decision)

Each filler bead must:
1. Pass live submit verification on a real listing (dry-run + one supervised live submit).
2. Have a fixture-based parser test for the operator's contact form HTML.
3. Be opt-in via the existing `apply_capable` registry — adding a filler automatically lights up auto-apply for that platform; no separate config flag needed.
4. Document the operator's known anti-bot/captcha quirks in a docstring.

## Status today (2026-05-07)
- Friendly error in place (UnsupportedPlatformError) — see PR closing FlatPilot-mrt.
- README documents the limitation in the Scope section.
- No code work needed unless this bead is unblocked.

title: Decide apply support for inberlinwohnen + immoscout24
description:
Auto-apply silently skips inberlinwohnen and immoscout24 flats today (auto_apply.completeness_ok returns False because no filler is registered for those platforms). Manual flatpilot apply <id> against either platform raises KeyError: 'No filler registered for platform …'. Neither behavior is documented as an intentional limitation.

Specifics:
- inberlinwohnen.de: cards on the Wohnungsfinder feed deeplink off-site to each operator's expose page (degewo / Howoge / Gesobau / etc.) — there is no single contact form; a real filler would need per-operator form recipes. Probably not worth building.
- ImmoScout24: integration is RSS-only by design (FlatPilot-l9hm and FlatPilot-h7q established that scraping the site is fragile). A filler would re-introduce the HTML fetch path RSS exists to avoid. Probably also not worth building.

Goal of this bead: make a deliberate decision (build vs document-as-unsupported), file follow-up beads for whichever option wins, and update README's 'Phases' section so users know up front that scrape+notify works for these platforms but apply does not.

Recommended outcome: document as unsupported, surface a friendlier error than KeyError when a user tries flatpilot apply <id> on one of these flats, close this bead. But the decision is the deliverable — not the implementation.
close_reason: Decision recorded; friendly error + README updated in PR #45. Follow-up reversal filed as FlatPilot-6pq (blocked).

title: Cleanups in flatpilot/auto_apply.py: type annotations + attachment-resolve logging
description:
Two minor cleanups flagged by the FlatPilot-f2fa final code review:

1. Add type annotations to run_pipeline_apply and _try_flat. Every other public function in flatpilot/auto_apply.py is annotated; these two were skipped. Use 'profile: Profile, console: Console' (Console from rich.console).

2. In _try_flat's completeness-failure branch, the 'except Exception: attachments = []' that re-resolves attachments for the auto_skipped row write swallows the exception silently. Replace with logger.warning('auto-apply: attachment re-resolve failed for flat %d (%s): %s', flat_id, platform, exc) so a flaky AttachmentError leaves a thread to pull on. logger is already in scope at module top.

title: Within-day pacing beyond cap × cooldown for auto-apply
description: Today FlatPilot's auto-apply uses a hard daily cap (default 20/platform) plus a per-submit cooldown (default 120s). Together they produce a coarse '20 max in ~40 min' burst pattern at start of day, then nothing for 23 hours. Some users may want a smoother cadence — e.g. 'max 1 auto-apply every 72 minutes' to spread submits across the day and look less robotic. Add a third optional config: cap_pacing_seconds_per_platform — when set, gate at MIN(cooldown, cap_pacing) so the cap effectively gets stretched. Out of scope for FlatPilot-f2fa per spec §8; revisit only if simple cap/cooldown proves insufficient.

The brainstorming for FlatPilot-f2fa picked semantic C1 (saved searches gate auto-apply only; notifications stay base-profile-routed). Some users will want C2: per-saved-search notification routing — e.g. 'Telegram me only for kreuzberg-2br matches, email me Spandau matches'. Schema-wise: dispatcher would key off matched_saved_searches_json (already on matches table) and the saved-search definition would need a notifications field analogous to Profile.notifications. Estimate ~+200 LOC.

Power users want: (a) add 2-3 named saved searches via prompts, (b) tune profile.auto_apply.daily_cap_per_platform and cooldown_seconds_per_platform interactively, (c) edit existing saved searches on re-run instead of skipping. Today re-running 'flatpilot init' with an existing 'auto-default' silently skips the prompt; this bead extends that path. Out-of-scope for FlatPilot-f2fa (kept the wizard narrow).

The dashboard renders auto/manual badges but does NOT yet expose a dropdown to filter Applied rows by saved-search name. Add a client-side filter (vanilla JS, like the existing district / rent-max filters in view.py) keyed on a data-saved-search attribute on each application row. No backend changes needed; the column is already populated for method='auto' rows.
