title: Evaluate graphify code-graph skill once Phase 5 polyglot lands
description:
Evaluate (and potentially install) graphify (https://github.com/safishamsi/graphify, PyPI: graphifyy) as a Claude Code skill once the codebase is polyglot enough to make the graph worth maintaining.

## Why this is parked, not done now
- Today FlatPilot is ~13k LOC of pure Python, ~45 src files. CLAUDE.md already documents the module layout; Claude can hold the architecture in its head with grep + Read.
- graphify's value scales with corpus size and modality. Their own benchmark shows 71x token reduction at 52 mixed files (code + papers + images) — the win on a small single-language repo is much smaller and likely not worth the maintenance overhead (git hook, regenerated graph on every commit, graphify-out/ artifact).
- The LinkedIn pitch ("understands UI ↔ K8s deployment connections") is a polyglot-stack feature. We don't have that stack yet.

## When to actually do it (any of)
1. Phase 5 lands (FlatPilot-0wfb): FastAPI backend + React/Next.js frontend = real polyglot moment. Code graph spanning Python ↔ TS gives Claude what greps can't.
2. Codebase grows past ~25k LOC and module boundaries blur.
3. We start ingesting non-code corpus — listing-site screenshots, scraped landlord-doc PDFs, Berlin rental-law research notes. Multi-modal is graphify's real edge.

## Setup (when triggered)
On a throwaway branch first — do NOT commit graphify-out/ or the git hook to main until it's earning its keep:
1. pip install graphifyy
2. graphify claude install
3. /graphify . --mode deep --wiki  (in a Claude Code session)
4. graphify hook install  (only if we decide to keep it)
5. Add graphify-out/ to .gitignore

## Acceptance criteria
- Run a representative Claude task (e.g. 'add new scraper that reuses session/backoff/dedup correctly') with and without graphify loaded.
- Compare: (a) input tokens used, (b) whether the answer correctly cited cross-module dependencies the graph would surface.
- Decision: keep only if measured token savings > graph-maintenance overhead OR if the agent demonstrably catches a cross-module break it would have missed.

## Related
Blocked by FlatPilot-0wfb (Phase 5 Web UI epic) as the primary trigger. File can be reopened earlier if criterion 2 or 3 hits first.

title: pyproject.toml: add pytest-cov to dev extras
description:
The dev optional-dependencies only include pytest>=8.0 and ruff>=0.3. There is no pytest-cov, so coverage reports are unavailable. Without coverage data it is impossible to know which branches and error-handling paths are actually exercised by the 233-test suite (the matcher filters, the dedup edge cases, and the filler submit paths are likely well-covered; the wizard and doctor branches are probably not).

Scope: add pytest-cov>=5.0 to [project.optional-dependencies] dev in pyproject.toml. Optionally add a [tool.pytest.ini_options] addopts entry of --cov=flatpilot --cov-report=term-missing so every pytest run shows a coverage summary. Do NOT set a coverage threshold gate yet — let the first run establish a baseline.

Acceptance: pip install -e '.[dev]' installs pytest-cov; pytest --cov=flatpilot produces a coverage report without errors; ruff passes; pytest 233→233.

title: view.py: conn parameter should be sqlite3.Connection | None, not Any
description:
generate_html(conn: Any = None) and _load_applications(conn: Any) in view.py annotate the sqlite connection parameter as Any, which defeats type checking. The parameter is always either None (in which case get_conn() is called) or a sqlite3.Connection. Using Any means a caller who passes the wrong type (e.g. a dict, a mock without the right interface) is silently accepted by mypy and only fails at runtime.

Scope: add import sqlite3 to view.py; change conn: Any to conn: sqlite3.Connection | None in generate_html and conn: sqlite3.Connection in _load_applications. Also remove Any from the import list if it's no longer used elsewhere in the file. ~5 LOC change.

Acceptance: ruff passes; after FlatPilot-chj (mypy), mypy reports no error on view.py's conn parameters; pytest 233→233.

title: tests/conftest.py: add berlin_profile pytest fixture
description:
Profile.load_example().model_copy(update={'city': 'Berlin'}) appears verbatim in at least 7 test sites across 5 test files (test_apply_orchestrator, test_apply_lock, test_inberlinwohnen_scraper x3, test_pipeline_backoff, test_kleinanzeigen_scraper). If load_example() or the example JSON schema changes, every callsite must be updated. This is classic pytest fixture material.

Scope: add a berlin_profile fixture to tests/conftest.py (3 lines). Replace the 7 inline callsites with the fixture. The fixture does NOT need tmp_db — it's a pure value, no filesystem side effects. Expected diff: +5 LOC in conftest.py, -14 LOC across 5 test files.

Acceptance: pytest 233→233; grep for 'load_example().model_copy' in tests/ returns 0 results.

title: Document FLATPILOT_APPLY_TIMEOUT_SEC + 409-on-lock-contention dashboard behavior
description:
Two recently-added apply behaviors are not documented in README.md / CLAUDE.md:

1. `FLATPILOT_APPLY_TIMEOUT_SEC` env var (added in PR #28 / FlatPilot-c3e). Default 180s, overrides the dashboard subprocess timeout for `flatpilot apply <id>`. Operators with slow Playwright runs (large attachments, slow networks) need to know the knob exists.

2. HTTP 409 response from POST /api/applications when the cross-process apply lock is held by another flatpilot apply process (added in PR #29 / FlatPilot-wsp). Both the in-process double-click path (PR #28 _inflight_flats) and the new cross-process lock-held path return 409 with a payload describing what's blocked.

Suggested location: README's 'Dashboard' / 'Configuration' sections, or CLAUDE.md's project conventions block. Two-line addition each.

title: Type-annotate `conn` param across apply.py + applications.py lock/record helpers
description:
Five lock/record helpers leave the `conn` parameter un-annotated: acquire_apply_lock + release_apply_lock (src/flatpilot/apply.py), and record_skip + record_response + _record_application (src/flatpilot/applications.py).

Opus reviewer on PR #28 deliberately did NOT add type annotations on the new lock primitives (FlatPilot-bxm) because it would have been inconsistent with the existing un-annotated pattern in applications.py — annotating only some helpers in the same code-cluster would be worse than the status quo.

A uniform sweep across all five callsites — annotate `conn: sqlite3.Connection` (or whichever wrapper the project standardizes on; see flatpilot/database.py for current return type) — in a single bead avoids the inconsistency. No behavior change; ruff/mypy-only diff.

title: Refine 409 toast wording in dashboard (Apply already in progress)
description:
The dashboard's Apply toast in src/flatpilot/view.py (around line 589-590) currently renders any non-2xx response as 'Apply failed:' followed by the payload. For HTTP 409 — both the in-process _inflight_flats double-click path added in PR #28 and the new cross-process lock-held path added in FlatPilot-wsp / PR #29 — the user-facing message would be more accurate as 'Apply already in progress — retry shortly.'

Surface area: purely the inline JS in view.py. Branch on the response status code (409) or on a payload signal. Pre-existing quirk from PR #28, but FlatPilot-wsp adds a second 409 flow so the wording mismatch now exercises in two distinct user paths.

Discovered by the final code-reviewer over feat/apply-lock-held-409 (PR #29). Low-impact UX polish.

title: Translate AlreadyAppliedError to HTTP 409 from dashboard subprocess output
description: When the dashboard spawns 'flatpilot apply N' and the new acquire_apply_lock raises AlreadyAppliedError because the cross-process lock is held, the CLI subprocess exits 1 (cli.py:581-583 yellow-prints and raises typer.Exit(1)). server._spawn_apply translates returncode != 0 to ok=False, so the dashboard returns HTTP 500. That is technically correct (the apply did not complete) but semantically wrong: 500 means 'unexpected server error', whereas the right status for 'apply already in progress, please retry' is 409 — same as the in-process _inflight_flats fast-path 409. Resolution: detect the 'apply already in progress' substring in stdout_tail, or have the CLI subprocess use a distinct exit code (e.g. 4) for AlreadyAppliedError that _spawn_apply maps to 409. The latter is cleaner — define an APPLY_LOCK_HELD_EXIT = 4 constant in flatpilot.apply, have cli.py exit with it on AlreadyAppliedError-from-lock-acquire, have server._spawn_apply / _handle_apply check returncode == 4 and respond 409. Discovered by the final code-reviewer over feat/apply-server-hardening-round-2.

title: Unify the apply-lock stale buffer constant across apply.py and server.py
description: Both layers of the apply concurrency story use the same buffer above apply_timeout_sec(): src/flatpilot/apply.py:117 hardcodes 'apply_timeout_sec() + 60' inside acquire_apply_lock for the apply_locks stale-row reaper, and src/flatpilot/server.py:128 declares _INFLIGHT_WATCHDOG_BUFFER_SEC = 60 used by _inflight_watchdog_threshold_sec(). Same architectural meaning, expressed twice — if a future tuning changes one, the other can drift silently. Resolution: expose a single constant (e.g. STALE_APPLY_BUFFER_SEC = 60) from flatpilot.apply, import it in server.py, and reference it from both call sites. Also worth a one-line cross-reference comment near each layer pointing at the other ('see flatpilot.apply.acquire_apply_lock for the matching DB-side reaper' / vice versa). Discovered by the final code-reviewer over feat/apply-server-hardening-round-2.

title: Clean up 14 pre-existing ruff errors in untouched modules
description:
Running '.venv/bin/ruff check .' against origin/main reports 14 errors across these files:

- src/flatpilot/__main__.py
- src/flatpilot/database.py
- src/flatpilot/log.py
- src/flatpilot/matcher/distance.py
- src/flatpilot/matcher/filters.py
- src/flatpilot/notifications/email.py
- src/flatpilot/notifications/telegram.py
- src/flatpilot/stats.py
- src/flatpilot/wizard/init.py

13 of the 14 are auto-fixable via 'ruff check --fix'. The other one (likely SIM102 nested-if in wizard/init.py:227) needs manual review.

Discovered while shipping the apply-server-hardening PR (PRs since at least #24 have shipped past these without flagging). They predate the current session's work and are not specific to any feature.

Resolution: open a small PR running 'ruff check --fix .' plus the one manual fix, verify the suite still passes, ship as a chore commit. Low risk because all 14 are stylistic (import sorting, single-if collapse, etc.).

Acceptance: '.venv/bin/ruff check .' reports zero errors on a clean checkout of main.

title: Watchdog for _inflight_flats slots that never release
description:
FlatPilot-cy9 (PR #N) added a server-side per-flat in-flight set wrapped in try/finally: cleanup runs as long as _spawn_apply returns or raises. FlatPilot-2yu's 180s subprocess timeout means _spawn_apply ALWAYS returns within 180s in production paths, so a slot can't leak indefinitely.

Risk: if some future refactor removes the subprocess timeout, or _spawn_apply gets replaced with an in-process call that genuinely hangs, _inflight_flats grows without bound and applies for the held flat are blocked until the dashboard process restarts.

Resolution: add a watchdog that times out a held slot after e.g. 5 minutes — log a warning, force-discard, and let the next request through. Or add a lighter assertion: cap |_inflight_flats| at e.g. 100 and raise on overflow so the bug surfaces loudly during dev.

Acceptance: a unit test that holds a slot beyond the watchdog timeout sees the slot freed automatically.

Defensive only; current code paths can't reach the failure mode.

title: Make APPLY_TIMEOUT_SEC configurable
description:
FlatPilot-2yu (PR #N) added a 180s timeout to the dashboard's _spawn_apply subprocess. The constant is hard-coded in src/flatpilot/server.py. A user with a slow network or many CAPTCHA-equivalent prompts may want longer; a paranoid user may want shorter.

Resolution: expose via the profile (profile.apply.timeout_sec, default 180) or an env var (FLATPILOT_APPLY_TIMEOUT_SEC). Keep 180 as the default — it's a reasonable upper bound for headed Playwright apply.

Acceptance: setting the configured value to e.g. 5 makes the timeout fire on a real apply that takes longer than 5s; default behaviour (no setting) keeps the 180s bound.

Trivial; can ride along with any future server.py change. Not urgent.

title: O. 'Connected accounts' page with per-platform Connect buttons
description:
Web UI page (under Epic O — FastAPI + React/Next.js) that lists every supported rental platform with a Connect / Reconnect button. Clicking Connect spawns a Playwright headed browser window pointed at the platform's login URL, the user logs in inside that controlled window (entering credentials directly into the platform's site — FlatPilot never sees them), the page detects login completion (URL leaves /login OR user clicks "Done"), captures cookies via storage_state(), saves them to ~/.flatpilot/sessions/<platform>/state.json, closes the window, and the UI updates to show the connection as active with an expiry date.

Why this UX framing rather than a literal OAuth-style redirect: WG-Gesucht / Kleinanzeigen / ImmoScout24 don't publish OAuth endpoints. There is no protocol-level way to make "sign in with the platform → callback to FlatPilot" work. The Playwright-controlled window is the workaround that delivers the same UX without requiring platform cooperation.

Backend: identical to the `flatpilot login <platform>` CLI command. This task is the UI layer on top of that engine — the headed-browser launching, cookie capture, and storage paths are shared. So the task here is mostly:
- Connections page React component (logo + status + Connect/Reconnect button per platform)
- FastAPI endpoint POST /connect/<platform> that spawns the browser via the same backend the CLI uses
- WebSocket or polling for the UI to learn when the connection completes

UI mockup:

  Connected accounts:
    ☑ WG-Gesucht        Connected · expires Mar 22  [Reconnect]
    □ Kleinanzeigen     Not connected               [Connect →]
    □ ImmoScout24       Not connected               [Connect →]

Depends on: Epic O having basic structure (FastAPI backend + React frontend scaffolding). Depends on the `flatpilot login` CLI shipping first because it owns the underlying mechanism.

Acceptance: from the deployed Web UI, clicking a Connect button opens a real headed browser window, lets the user log in to the actual rental platform, and on completion the UI shows the platform as connected without ever transmitting the user's password to FlatPilot.

title: Set up pytest suite + initial test coverage
description:
Why this issue exists
---------------------
CLAUDE.md lists pytest as the test framework ('focus on matcher logic
and scraper parsers with fixture HTML'), but the repo has no tests/
directory. Every change through Phase 1 was validated by manual
Docker smoke runs only. That worked for a one-person project at MVP
stage, but as the codebase grows (I-epic Kleinanzeigen, I-bis dedup,
L-epic Apply flow, N-epic Auto-Apply with safety rails) the surface
area that can silently regress is growing fast.

Noticed while shipping FlatPilot-knf (PR #16): a symmetric code path
I didn't exercise manually (SMTP 'enabled but missing') shipped
without coverage because there was no fast way to assert the
branches. Also introduced a subtle regression window — doctor now
calls load_profile() and will crash on a malformed profile.json — that
a single unit test would have caught at author time.

What needs to be done
---------------------
Minimum viable scope (split into sub-tasks if the work is >1 PR):

1. Infrastructure
   - Create tests/ with pytest config in pyproject.toml
   - conftest.py with fixtures for a scratch FLATPILOT_DIR (tmp_path-based)
     and an example Profile (leveraging Profile.load_example())
   - Update pyproject 'dev' extras to include pytest, pytest-cov; wire
     up 'ruff + pytest' as the quality gate used by CI and the README
     Build-and-Test block

2. Initial coverage targets (in priority order)
   - matcher/filters.py — deterministic filters are the domain core;
     every filter should have edge-case unit tests (rent band, rooms,
     WBS, distance, furnished_pref, contract length, pets, smoker)
   - matcher/distance.py — Haversine + Nominatim adapter (geocoding
     cache hit/miss), with httpx mock
   - scrapers/wg_gesucht.py — parse() against saved fixture HTML
     (resources/tests/wg_gesucht/*.html); covers city-ID lookup,
     selector resilience, URL construction
   - doctor.py — all branches of _check_telegram / _check_smtp
     (including malformed profile)
   - notifications/dispatcher.py — the profile-hash scoping logic
     introduced in FlatPilot-usm

3. CI
   - Add a GitHub Actions workflow that runs ruff + pytest on every PR
   - Fail the workflow on test failure or < some floor coverage
     (e.g. 60% to start, raise over time)

Why P4
------
Phase 1 is live and working. Tests are an investment in velocity, not
a fix for a user-visible bug. File now so we can pick it up between
feature epics (good 'cooldown' work) rather than forgetting the gap.

title: O. Web UI (multi-user, Phase 5)
description: FastAPI backend + React/Next.js frontend. Per-user profiles, magic-link auth, SQLite -> Postgres migration path.

title: O3. React/Next.js tabs
description: Port Matches / Applied / Responses tabs from the HTML dashboard.

title: O4. Docker-compose + deployment docs
description: One-command local stack; deployment instructions for a VPS.

title: O1. Architecture decision record
description: Decide: FastAPI + Next.js; magic-link auth; SQLite -> Postgres migration path; deployment story (docker-compose starter).

title: O2. Per-user isolation in data model
description: All tables gain user_id; row-level scoping; migration from single-user state.

value: Never push directly to main. Every change goes through a PR: git fetch origin; git checkout -b <type>/<slug> origin/main; commit; git push -u origin <branch>; gh pr create --base main --head <branch> --fill. Return the PR URL and stop. Force-push allowed only on personal feature branches that nobody else has pulled (--force-with-lease).

After a FlatPilot PR merges, DO NOT delete the local or remote feature branch, and DO NOT drop stashes, unless the user explicitly asks. Why: user's standing preference to preserve merged branches for historical reference (e.g. feat/l2-anschreiben-composer was called out as hands-off in the handoff). How to apply: after merge, only sync main (git checkout main && git pull --ff-only) and stop. Do not run 'git branch -d', 'git push --delete', 'git stash drop', or any similar cleanup without an explicit request. Treat 'cleanup' as scope the user opts into, not a default step.

Clean-room policy: FlatPilot is independent MIT-licensed. Never copy code from any other project (including any job-hunting agent that may have inspired the high-level architecture). Read outside code only as a design reference; always rewrite from scratch.

FlatPilot is currently single-user CLI (Phase 3). Long-term direction: convert to small multi-user web app — 'Option C' scope — for friends-of-friends, no public marketing, no payments. Strict sequencing: complete Phase 3 CLI for the developer's own use FIRST, then validate by using it personally on real WG-Gesucht searches for 1-2 weeks, THEN start Epic O (Web UI / FlatPilot-0wfb). Do NOT start FastAPI / React / Postgres work until Phase 3 is shipped end-to-end and personally validated. The CLI work is not throwaway — every Python module becomes a backend module the FastAPI app calls later. Phase 5 will need: Hetzner/Fly hosting, Postgres migration, FastAPI backend, React frontend, magic-link email auth, browser extension OR bookmarklet for per-user platform cookie capture. Decided in PR #18 session 2026-04-22.

When the user's prompt has a 'Possible ride-alongs' section that explicitly lists candidates for inline inclusion, treat each listed item as a TASK ITEM, not just background reading. Add it to the working checklist before starting implementation, and verify each one is either (a) included in the PR or (b) explicitly justified as skipped before declaring the work done. Why: in PR #30 (FlatPilot-etu) the user explicitly suggested 'A README note about what flatpilot scrape --platform inberlinwohnen now ingests on fresh install → ride along inline' and I dropped it; only caught it when the user asked 'do we have any more related PR tasks'. How to apply: when reading any FlatPilot prompt, scan for 'ride-along' / 'inline' / 'fix inline' suggestions, list them in your initial pitch back to the user, and tick them off as you go.

Commit author for this repo: Mukhammad Ibrokhimov <ibrohimovmuhammad2020@gmail.com>. In a fresh clone: git config user.email ibrohimovmuhammad2020@gmail.com && git config user.name 'Mukhammad Ibrokhimov'. Any other email on the machine is NOT for commits here.

For FlatPilot: one PR per epic. If an epic has >10 tasks, split into multiple PRs (thematic split, topologically ordered). Otherwise the whole epic lands in one PR — one commit per task, each commit message referencing its beads ID. Small leftover tasks from completed sub-areas can ride along if they fit the theme. Why: the user set this rule after observing that per-task PRs were noise and mixed-theme mega-PRs were hard to review. How to apply: before claiming the first task in an epic, count the epic's open tasks with 'bd list' filtered to that epic; if >10, decide on a thematic split (e.g. 'matcher core' vs 'matcher CLI') and declare it in the first PR description. Each PR closes every beads task it contains.
No AI co-author trailers in commits. No 'Co-Authored-By: Claude ...', no '🤖 Generated with Claude Code'. Commits are authored by the human only.

Never store user passwords for rental platforms (WG-Gesucht, Kleinanzeigen, ImmoScout24, future) anywhere in FlatPilot — not in profile.json, .env, or any other file. Reasons: (1) Docker container can't reach host OS keyring, leaving only file storage which is unsafe for credentials; (2) programmatic credential login is a strong anti-bot signal that risks getting accounts flagged; (3) 2FA / captchas still require human intervention even with stored passwords; (4) ToS posture is much worse than cookie reuse from a real human session. Solution: cookies-only auth. CLI version (FlatPilot-5b8) uses headed Playwright + 'press Enter when logged in' UX. Future multi-user Web UI (FlatPilot-8jx) uses browser extension or bookmarklet to capture cookies from the user's normal browser session — never asks for or stores passwords. None of WG-Gesucht / Kleinanzeigen / ImmoScout24 publish OAuth endpoints, so a literal 'Sign in with Platform' redirect-and-callback flow is structurally impossible. Decided in PR #18 session 2026-04-22.
